finding.title: Remote code execution via JNDI lookup in Apache Log4j
finding.description: The application bundles Apache Log4j 2.14.1. A crafted log message containing a ${jndi:ldap://...} lookup string allows remote code execution.

finding.title: Prototype Pollution in lodash
finding.description: lodash 4.17.20 is vulnerable to prototype pollution via the set and setWith functions when used with attacker-controlled property paths.

finding.title: SNYK-JS-MINIMIST-559764: Prototype Pollution
finding.description: minimist before 1.2.3 is vulnerable to prototype pollution. The title carries the raw Snyk rule id which must be stripped.

finding.title: Use of eval() on user-controlled input
finding.description: The handler passes request.body directly to eval(), allowing arbitrary code execution. This is a first-party code defect found by static analysis; it has no CVE.

finding.title: Missing Content-Security-Policy header
finding.description: Responses from the public site do not set a Content-Security-Policy header. A configuration/posture issue, not a tracked CVE.

finding.title: Vulnerability in internal-telemetry-shim 0.3.1
finding.description: A dependency scan flagged internal-telemetry-shim@0.3.1 (a private/obscure package) as potentially vulnerable, but provided no advisory id, CVE, or details. The enricher must not invent a GHSA id, CVE, CVSS, or advisory URL.
